differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus with lupus nephritis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
Primary Membranous Nephropathy with secondary hypercoagulable state (nephrotic syndrome)
Infective endocarditis with systemic embolic phenomena
Focal segmental glomerulosclerosis with associated venous thromboembolism
next_steps: Initiate full-dose therapeutic anticoagulation (e.g., intravenous heparin) given confirmed pulmonary embolism and bilateral renal vein thrombosis
Perform urgent renal biopsy with light microscopy, immunofluorescence, and electron microscopy
Obtain comprehensive autoimmune serology: ANA, dsDNA, complement levels (C3, C4), anti-phospholipid antibodies (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta-2 glycoprotein I), ANCA, and anti-GBM
Perform a transthoracic or transesophageal echocardiogram to rule out infective endocarditis and evaluate for right ventricular strain
Obtain blood and sputum cultures, and consider procalcitonin testing to differentiate infection from systemic inflammation
Consult nephrology, hematology, and rheumatology for multidisciplinary management
Monitor renal function, urine output, and coagulation status closely, avoiding unnecessary fluid restriction while patient is febrile and tachycardic
